page: UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server configured with 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system features a KVM hypervisor and a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.0 GB per core. Acceleration is delivered via two NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a combined 96 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage and comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, as well as Geekbench single-core execution. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%. In database and key-value operations, the server achieves top-tier status, processing 127.0 million Redis SET operations per second and scoring in the top 10% for Passmark database operations. Large language model inference benchmarks show top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds for small 135M models, strong performance for medium 7B models, and average performance for large 70B models. Ray tracing performance on Geekbench falls within the average range.

The server offers high resource density with its dual-GPU configuration and substantial memory capacity, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring parallel GPU acceleration without the overhead of dedicated CPU allocations. However, the shared CPU allocation and the complete lack of local storage represent notable architectural tradeoffs that require external storage solutions. This configuration is suited for LLM inference of small to medium models, data processing, database operations, and parallelized GPU-compute workloads.
description: UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor on a KVM hypervisor with shared CPU allocation. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM and two NVIDIA L40S GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering 96 GB of total VRAM. The server does not include local storage. In benchmarks, it demonstrates top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU performance, alongside top-tier Redis SET operations and small-model LLM inference speeds. Large-model inference and ray tracing benchmarks perform at average levels. Given the shared CPU allocation and lack of local storage, this instance is designed for GPU-accelerated workloads, medium-scale LLM inference, and database operations that leverage external storage.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA L40S GPUs. It delivers top-tier CPU and database performance but lacks local storage.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-2xL40S features 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs. Optimized for GPU computing and database workloads.
tagline: A dual-GPU accelerated server featuring AMD EPYC processors and substantial memory for parallel computing and machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB total VRAM for accelerated computing
32 AMD EPYC 9575F physical cores with 384 GB of system memory
Top-tier CPU, database, and small-model LLM inference benchmark performance
Shared CPU allocation model on a KVM hypervisor
No local storage included, requiring external storage configurations
Suited for machine learning inference, database operations, and parallel processing
